question: Following the Camunda BPMN Framework convention, an `invoice` process has exactly one start event and it is modelled as a message start event so the diagram can show the collaboration with the surrounding human flow. How can clients start an instance?

options.text: Either via `CreateProcessInstance` against the BPMN process ID or by publishing the named message — both APIs work.
Only by publishing the named message; `CreateProcessInstance` is rejected because the process has no none start event.
You must add an explicit none start event in parallel — the engine cannot start a process whose only start event is a message event.
The engine starts a new instance on every deployment because there is only one start event.

a.text: The docs state that if there is exactly one message start event for the whole process definition, it can also be treated as if it were a none start event when starting an instance — so both `CreateProcessInstance` by BPMN ID and `PublishMessage` work.

b.text: `CreateProcessInstance` against the BPMN process ID is explicitly allowed in this single-message-start case; it is not rejected.

c.text: The engine has no problem with a sole message start event; no parallel none start event is required for clients to start the process.

d.text: Deployment never auto-starts process instances in Zeebe; start events are triggered by API calls, messages, timers or signals, not by deployment events.

explanation: The Camunda BPMN Framework convention is to use message start events even when a process has only one start, so the diagram can communicate the collaboration with the surrounding human flow. To avoid burdening clients, the engine treats a sole message start event as equivalent to a none start event for instantiation — so either API works.

docs.title: Routing events to processes - best practices
